title: Designing Intuitive Dashboards for Hybrid Quantum-Classical Operations
why:
- Hybrid quantum-classical workflows, involving both quantum and classical processors, are already being used in enterprises, not just discussed in theory.
- IBM’s LSF can coordinate hybrid-classical workflows across both quantum and classical (x86) systems, with QPUs working alongside GPUs and CPUs to expand HPC capabilities in tasks like optimization and quantum chemistry.
- The Quantum Machines OPX1000 enables real-time orchestration, mid-circuit measurements, and quantum feedback on QPUs within hundreds of nanoseconds, while supporting layers handle calibrations, optimizations, and error correction in microseconds.
- Operations teams face significant challenges using fragmented dashboards, often needing to consult multiple platforms or handwritten notes to diagnose and optimize hybrid quantum-classical jobs.
- Effective dashboards for hybrid quantum-classical operations require transparent device and job statistics, resource-focused health metrics, and telemetry per job and qubit to facilitate deeper analysis and improved workflow scheduling.
